hadiths.ar:
603  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ ر َّي ْذِ ن ْن ِ عَّلِى ِّ ن ْن ِ إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ: سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن ِى  سَّي ِّذَّ إَّلْعَّان ِذِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّق ُّلْث ُّ  
لَّهُّ ي َّا إ َّن َّث ِ إ َّج ْن ِزْن ِى  عَّن ْ ج َّذِّن َّا رَّسُّولِ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ لَّم َّا عُّزِح َّ ن ِهِ إ ِلَّى إَّلس َّمَّ اءِ وَّ إ َّمَّ زَّةُّ رَّن  ُّهُّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ن ِح َّمْسِي ن َّ 
صَّلاَّة ً كَّي ْف َّ لَّمْ ي َّسْا َّلْهُّ إَّلي  َّح ْق ِي ف َّ عَّن ْ إ ُّم َّي ِهِ جَّي  َّى ق َّالَّ لَّهُّ مُّوسَّى ن ْن ُّ عِمْزَّإن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إِرْ ج ِغْ إ ِلَّى رَّن ِّك َّ ق َّاسْا َّلْهُّ 
إَّلي  َّح ْق ِي ف َّ ق َّا ِن  َّ إ ُّم َّي َّك َّ لأَّ ي ُّطِي ق ُّ ذ َّلِك َّ ق َّق َّالَّ  «
ق َّلاَّ ي ُّزَّإج ِعُّهُّ ق ِى  س َّى ْءٍ ي َّا ْمُّزُّةُّ ن ِهِ ق َّلَّم َّا سَّا َّلَّهُّ مُّوسَّى عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ذ َّلِك َّ وَّ صَّارَّ س َّق ِي عاً لِأ ُّم َّي ِ هِ إ ِلَّي ْهِ لَّمْ  ي َّح ُّز ْ لَّهُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّزُّذ َّ  
س َّق َّاعَّه َّ إ َّج ِي هِ مُّوسَّى عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّزَّج َّغَّ إ ِلَّى رَّن ِّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ق َّسَّا َّلَّهُّ إَّلي  َّح ْق ِي ف َّ إ ِلَّى إ َّن ْ رَّذ َّ هَّا إ ِلَّى ج َّمْسِ صَّلَّوَّإب ٍ 
ق َّق ُّلْث ُّ لَّهُّ ي َّا إ َّن َّث ِ ق َّلِمَّ لَّمْ ي َّزْج ِغْ إ ِلَّى رَّن ِّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ  وَّ لَّمْ ي َّسْا َّلْهُّ إَّلي  َّح ْق ِي ف َّ مِن ْ ج َّمْسِ صَّلَّوَّإب ٍ وَّ ق َّذْ سَّا َّلَّهُّ مُّوسَّى عَّلَّي ْهِ 
إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّزْج ِغَّ إ ِلَّى رَّن ِّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ وَّ   ي َّسْا َّلَّهُّ إَّلي  َّح ْق ِي ف َّ ق َّق َّال 
مَّغَّ إ َّج ْزِ ج َّمْسِي ن َّ صَّلاَّة ً لِق َّوْلِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل   إ َّ لأَّ ي َّزَّى إ َّن  َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ لَّم َّا 
هَّن َّظَّ إ ِلَّى إَّلْأ َّرْض ِ ن َّز َّلَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ ج َّن ْزَّن ِي لُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّق َّالَّ  ي َّا مُّحَّم َّذُّ إ ِن  َّ رَّن  َّك َّ ي ُّق ْزِن ُّك َّ إَّلس َّلاَّمَّ وَّ ي َّق ُّولُّ لَّك َّ إ ِن  َّهَّا ج َّمْس   
ن ِح َّمْسِي ن ۝  مٰا ي ُّن َّذ َّلُّ إَّلْق َّوْلُّ لَّذَّى  َّ وَّ مٰا إ َّن َّا ن ِط َّلاّٰمٍ لِلْعَّن ِي ذِ  ۝»
ي ُّوصَّف ُّ ن ِمَّكَّان ٍ ق َّق َّال   إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ لِزَّسُّولِ إَّلل َّهِ 
صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ  إ لِهِ إِرْج ِغْ إ ِلَّى رَّن ِّك َّ ق َّق َّال س َّلاَّم ۝  إ ِن ِّى  ذ ٰإهِث   إ ِلىٰ رَّن ِّى  
سَّي َّهْذِي ن ِ   ۝»: «۝  وَّ عَّح ِلْث ُّ إ ِلَّي ْك َّ رَّب ِّ لِي َّزْص ىٰ  ۝»  وَّ مَّعْن َّى ق َّوْلِهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل 
«۝  ق َّق ِز ُّوإ إ ِلَّى إَّللّٰهِ   ۝» ي َّعْن ِى  جُّح  ُّوإ إ ِلَّى ن َّي ْث ِ إَّلل َّهِ ي َّا ن ُّن َّى  َّ إ ِن  َّ إَّلْكَّعْن َّه َّ ن َّي ْث ُّ إَّلل َّهِ ق َّمَّن ْ جَّح  َّ ن َّي ْث َّ إَّلل َّهِ ق َّق َّذْ ق َّص ذَّ إ ِلَّى 
إَّلل َّهِ وَّ إَّلْمَّسَّاج ِذَّ ن ُّي ُّوب ُّ إَّلل َّهِ ق َّمَّن ْ سَّعَّى إ ِلَّي ْهَّا ق َّق َّذْ سَّعَّى إ ِلَّى إَّلل َّهِ وَّ ق َّصَّذَّ إ ِلَّي ْهِ وَّ إَّلْمُّصَّل ى  مَّا ذَّإمَّ ق ِى  صَّلاَّي ِهِ ق َّهُّوَّ وَّإق ِف   
ن َّي ْن َّ ي َّذَّى ِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ق َّا ِن  َّ لِل َّهِ ي َّن َّارَّك َّ  وي َّعَّالَّى  ن ِق َّاعاً ق ِى  سَّمَّاوَّإي ِهِ ق َّمَّن ْ عُّزِح َّ ن ِهِ إ ِلَّى ن ُّق ْعَّه ٍ مِن ْهَّا ق َّق َّذْ عُّزِح َّ ن ِهِ إ ِلَّي ْهِ إ َّ 
لأَّ ي َّسْمَّغُّ إَّلل َّهَّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ي َّق ُّول إَّلْمَّلاٰن ِكَّه ُّ وَّ إَّلز ُّوحُّ إ ِلَّي ْهِ   وَّ ي َّق ُّولُّ إَّلل َّهُّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ق ِى  ق ِص َّه ِ عِي سَّى إِن ْن ِ مَّزْي َّمَّ 
عَّلَّي ْهَّا إَّلس َّلاَّم  
«۝ إ ِلَّي ْهِ ي َّصْعَّذُّ إَّلْكَّلِمُّ إَّلط َّي ِّث ُّ وَّ إَّلْعَّمَّلُّ إَّلصّٰالِحُّ ي َّزْق َّعُّهُّ ۝» 
وق ذ إ ج زج ث  هذ إ إلحذي ث  مسن ذإ ق ى  كي اب  إلمعارح . وإلصلاة  ق ى  إلي وم وإللي له  إ جذى وج مسون  ركعه ، من ها إلق زي ص ه  سن غ  
عس زة  ركعه  إلط هز إ رن غ ركعاب  وهى  إ ول صلاة  ق زص ها إلله عز  وج ل، وإلعصز إ رن غ ركعاب ، وإلمع زب  ي لاب  ركعاب ، وإلعس اء 
إلأ ج زة  إ رن غ ركعاب ، وإلع ذإة  ركعي ان ، ق هذ ة سن غ عس زة  ركعه  ق زي ص ه  وما سوى ذ لك  سن ه  ون اق له ، ولأ ي ي  م إلق زإن ض  إ لأ ن ها، إ ما 
ن اق له  إلط هزي ن  ق سث  عس زة  ركعه ، ون اق له  إلمع زب  إ رن غ ركعاب  ن عذها ن ي سلي مي ي ن ، وإ ما إلزكعي ان  ن عذ إلعس اء إلأ ج زة  من  ج لوس 
ق ا ن هما ي عذإن  ن زكعه ، ق ا ن  إ صاب  إلزج ل جذب  ق ن ل إ ن  ي ذرك  إ ج ز إللي ل وي صلى  إلوي ز ي كون   ق ذ ن اب  على إلوي ز ، وإ ذ إ إ ذرك  إ ج ز 
إللي ل صلى إلوي ز ن عذ صلاة  إللي ل.
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said: "When the Messenger of Allah {saws}, was taken on the Night 
Journey (Miraj), his Lord {AZJ} commanded him with fifty prayers.  
As he passed by each of the prophets, none of them asked him anything until he 
reached Musa ibn Imran {a.s}.  
Musa {a.s} asked: 'What did your Lord {AZJ} command you?'  
He {saws} replied: 'Fifty prayers.'  
He (Musa {a.s}) requested: 'Ask your Lord {AZJ} for a reduction, for your Ummah will not be 
able to bear that.'  
So He {saws} asked his Lord {AZJ}, and it was reduced by ten.  
Then He passed by the Prophets again, one by one, without them asking Him anything, 
until He came to Musa {a.s}, son of Imran, who (again) asked: "What did your Lord {AZJ} 
command you?"  
He {saws} said: "Forty prayers."  
He (Musa {a.s}) requested: "Ask your Lord {AZJ} for a relief, for your nation cannot bear 
that."  
So He asked his Lord {AZJ}, and it was reduced by ten.  
Then He {saws} passed by the Prophets again, one by one, and they did not ask him 
anything until he reached Musa {a.s}, son of Imran, who (again) asked: "What did your 
Lord {AZJ} command you?"  
He {saws} said: "Thirty prayers,"  
He (Musa {a.s}) requested: "Ask your Lord {AZJ} for a relief, for your nation cannot bear 
that."  
So He {saws} asked his Lord {AZJ}, and it was reduced by ten.  
Then He {saws} passed by the Prophets again, one by one, and they did not ask him 
anything until he reached Musa {a.s}, son of Imran, who (again) asked: "What did your 
Lord {AZJ} command you?"  
He {saws} said: "Twenty prayers."  
He (Musa {a.s}) requested: "Ask your Lord {AZJ} for a relief, for your nation cannot bear 
that."  
So He {saws} asked his Lord {AZJ}, and it was reduced by ten.  
Then He {saws} passed by the Prophets again, one by one, and they did not ask him 
anything until he reached Musa {a.s}, son of Imran, who (again) asked: "What did your 
Lord {AZJ} command you?"  
He {saws} said: "Ten prayers."  
He (Musa {a.s}) requested: "Ask your Lord {AZJ} for a relief, for your nation cannot bear 
that."  
Musa {a.s} continued: "I came to the people of Bani Israel with what Allah {SWT}, the 
Exalted, had made obligatory upon them, but they did not accept it or agree to it."  
So Prophet {saws}, asked his Lord {AZJ}, the Exalted, and it was reduced further for him, 
making it five.  
Then He {saws} passed by the Prophets again, one by one, and they did not ask him 
anything until he reached Musa {a.s}, son of Imran, who (again) asked: "What did your 
Lord {AZJ} command you?"  
He {saws} said: "Five prayers."  
He (Musa {a.s}) requested: "Ask your Lord {AZJ} for a relief, for your nation cannot bear 
that."  
He {saws} replied: 'I feel shy to ask my Lord {AZJ} again.'  
And thus the Messenger of Allah {saws} returned with the command for five prayers.  
The Messenger of Allah {saws} said: 'May Allah {SWT} reward Musa ibn Imran with good on 
behalf of my Ummah’.  
And Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} said: "'May Allah {SWT} reward Musa ibn 
Imran with good on our behalf”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Volume 1, p. 197 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 4, p. 13 • Tafsir Nur Al -
Thaqalayn, Part 3, p. 112 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.ar:
604 - وَّق َّال إَّلن  َّن ِى  ُّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ : « « ق َّلاَّ ي َّن ِي ي َّن  َّ إ ِلأ َّ ن ِوَّي ْزٍ .  
وصلاة  إللي ل ي مان ى  ركعاب  وإلس ق غ ركعي ان 
وصلى إلوي ز مغ صلاة  إللي ل لم ي عذ إلزكعي ي ن  من  ج لوس ن عذ إلعس اء إلأ ج زة  س ي ن ا، وكان ث  إلصلاة  له ق ى  إلي وم و إللي له  ج مسي ن   
ركعه ، وإ ن ما صارب  ج مسي ن  ركعه  لأن  ساع اب  إللي ل إي ن ي ا عس زة  ساعه  وساعاب  إلن هار إي ن ي ا عس زة  ساعه ، وق ي ما ن ي ن  طلوع 
إلق ح ز إ لى طلوع إلس مس ساعه   ق ح عل إلله عز  وج ل لكل ساعه  ركعي ي ن .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Zaid ibn Ali ibn al-Hussain {a.s} who said:  
"I asked my father, the Master of the Worshippers (Imam Ali ibn Al-Hussain {a.s}), saying,  
'O’ my father, inform me about our grandfather, the Messenger of Allah {saws}, when he 
was taken up to the heavens and his Lord {AZJ} commanded him with fifty prayers. Why 
did he not ask for a reduction for his community until Musa {a.s} said to him, "Return to 
your Lord {AZJ} and ask for a reduction, for your community will not be able to bear this"?' 
Imam Ali ibn Al -Hussain {a.s} said: 'O’ my son, the Messenger of Allah {saws} does not 
propose or make demands from his Lord {AZJ}, nor does he dispute any c ommand He 
gives him. However, when Musa {a.s} suggested it and became an intercessor on behalf 
of his community, it was not appropriate for him to refuse the intercession of his 
brother Musa. So he returned to his Lord {AZJ} and asked for a reduction until it was 
reduced to five prayers.' 
I then said: 'O’ my father, why did he not return to his Lord {AZJ} and ask for a reduction 
from five prayers, even though Musa had suggested that?'  
Imam {a.s} said: 'O’ my son, The Prophet {saws} wished to ensure a lightening of the burden 
while preserving the reward of fifty prayers for his community, as Allah {SWT}, the Mighty 
and Majestic, says, " Whoever brings a good deed shall have ten times its like " (Suran 
Al-An’am 6:160).  
Do you not see that when he returned to the earth, Archangel Jibril {a.s} descended to 
him and said: "O’ Muhammad, your Lord {AZJ} sends you greetings and says: Indeed, 
they are five (prayers) equating to fifty (in reward). No word is changed with Me, and I 
am not unjust to My servants."' 
I said: 'O’ my father, is not Allah {SWT}, the Glorified, beyond being confined to a place?' 
Imam {a.s} said: 'Yes, Allah {SWT} is far above such limitations.' 
I then asked: 'So what is the meaning of Musa’s statement to the Messenger of Allah 
{saws}, "Return to your Lord {AZJ}"?' 
Imam {a.s} said: 'It means the same as Ibrahim’s statement {a.s}:  
"Indeed, I will go to my Lord {AZJ}; He will guide me" (Surah As-Saffat 37:99),  
and Musa’s statement: "I hastened to You, my Lord {AZJ}, so that You might be pleased" 
(Surah Taha 20:84),  
and Allah’s {SWT} words: "So flee to Allah {SWT}" (Surah Adh-Dhariyat 51:50), which means 
pilgrimage to the House of Allah {SWT}. 
O’ my son, the Kaaba is the House of Allah {SWT}, and whoever performs Hajj to the 
House of Allah {SWT} has sought Allah {SWT}. The mosques are the Houses of Allah {SWT}, so 
whoever strives toward them strives toward Allah {SWT} and intends Him.  
While praying, one is standing in the presence of Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic. 
Verily, Allah {SWT}, the Blessed and Exalted, has realms in His heavens, and whoever is 
taken up to one of these realms is taken up to Him.  
Do you not hear Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, say: " The angels and the spirit 
ascend to Him" (Surah Al-Ma’arij 70:4) and in the story of Isa (Jesus {a.s}):  
"But Allah {SWT} raised him to Himself " (Surah An -Nisa 4:158), and He says: " To Him 
ascend good words, and He raises righteous deeds" (Surah Fatir 35:10).' 
[AL SADUQ] 
This hadith has been narrated with a chain of transmission in the book of Al -Ma'arij. 
Additionally, daily and nightly prayers are fifty -one units: the obligatory prayers are 
seventeen units –  
Dhuhr (noon) is four units and was the first prayer Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, 
made obligatory; Asr (afternoon) is four units; Maghrib (sunset) is three units; Isha 
(night) is four units; And Fajr (morning) is two units.  
The remaining are recommended (nafilah) prayers that complement and complete the 
obligatory prayers. 
As for the voluntary prayers for the Dhuhr and Asr, they total sixteen units, and the 
voluntary prayer for Maghrib consists of four units afterward with two salaams 
(salutations). The two units after the Isha prayer while sitting are counted as one unit.  
And If a person, for some reason or event cannot wait till reaching the end of the night 
(for Witr prayer) and prays the Witr early, they have (the rewards of) spent the night in 
Witr prayer. If they can access the end of the night, they should pray the Wi tr after the 
Salat Al Layl. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Volume 1, p. 198 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 67 • Tafsir Al -Burhan, vol. 
3, p. 487 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 3, p. 113 • Tafseer Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 7, p. 321  
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.ar:
605   -  وَّق َّال ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ ن ْن ُّ إ َّعْي َّن َّ ق َّالَّ إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : «
وَّ ق ِي هِن  َّ إَّلْق ِزَّإءَّة ُّ وَّ لَّي ْسَّ ق ِي هِن  َّ وَّهْم  ي َّعْن ِى  سَّهْو  ق َّز َّإذَّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ   سَّن ْعاً وَّ ق ِي هِن  َّ إَّلس َّهْوُّ وَّ لَّي ْسَّ 
ق ِي هِن  َّ  إَّلْق ِزَّإءَّة ُّ ق َّمَّن ْ س َّك  َّ ق ِى  إَّلْأ َّو َّلَّي َّي ْن ِ إ َّعَّاذَّ جَّي  َّى ي َّحْق َّظ َّ وَّ ي َّكُّون َّ عَّلَّى ي َّق ِي ن ٍ وَّ مَّن ْ س َّك  َّ ق ِى  إَّلْأ َّ ج ِي زَّي َّي ْن ِ عَّمِلَّ ن ِالْوَّهْمِ .
hadiths.en:
The Prophet, said:  
"Whoever believes in Allah {SWT} and the Last Day should not sleep except in a state of 
Witr (having performed the Witr prayer)”.  
[AL SADUQ] 
Night prayer consists of eight units (rak'ahs), two units of Shaf' (pair) prayer, one unit 
of Witr, and two units before Fajr, making a total of fifty-one units of prayer in a day and 
night.  
Whoever reaches the last part of the night and performs the Witr prayer along with the 
night prayer does not need to count the two units performed while sitting after the last 
Isha prayer. The prayers, therefore, amount to fifty units in a day and night. I t is fifty 
units because there are twelve hours in the night and twelve hours in the day, and 
between the rising of the dawn and the rising of the sun, there is one hour.  
Thus, Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, ordained two units for each hour.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 200 • Ilal al-Sharai’ Vol. 2, p. 330 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, 
Part 2, p. 341 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 98 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 94 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 95 • Wasail Al-
Shia, Vol. 4, p. 95 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 84, p. 145 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.ar:
606  -  وَّق َّال ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ وَّ إَّلْق ُّص َّي ْلُّ :  ق ُّلْن َّا لِأ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّ إ َّ رَّإ َّي ْث َّ ق َّوْلَّ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل ۝  إ ِن  َّ إَّلص َّلاٰة َّ كٰان َّث ْ عَّلَّى إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ 
كِي ٰان اً مَّوْق ُّوي اً   ۝»  ق َّال  
صَّلاَّة ً مُّو َّذ َّإة ً وَّ لَّوْ كَّان َّ ذ َّلِك َّ كَّذ َّلِك َّ لَّهَّلَّك َّ سُّلَّي ْمَّان ُّ ن ْن ُّ ذَّإوُّذَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ جِي ن َّ صَّلا َّهَّا ن ِع َّي ْ زِ  وَّق ْي ِهَّا وَّ لَّكِن  َّهُّ مَّي َّى مَّا 
ذ َّكَّزَّهَّا صَّلا َّهَّا .   
ق ال مصن ف  هذ إ إلكي اب : إ ن  إلح هال من  إ هل إلح لاف  ي ز عمون  إ ن  سلي مان  علي ه إلسلام إس ي ع ل ذ إب  ي وم ن عزض  إلح ي ل جي ى  
ي وإرب  إلس مس ن الحح اب ، ي م إ مز ن زذ إلح ي ل وإ مز ن ص زب  سوق ها وإ عن اق ها وق ي لها، وق ال: إ ن ها س ع لي ن ى  عن  ذ كز رن ى ، ولي س 
كما ي ق ولون  ج ل ن ن ى  إلله سلي مان  علي ه إلسلام عن  مي ل هذ إ إلق عل ل أ ن ه لم ي كن  للح ي ل ذ ن ث  ق ي ص زب  سوق ها وإ عن اق ها لأ ن ها لم  
ي عزض  ن ق سها علي ه ولم ي س ع له وإ ن ما عزص ث  علي ه وهى  ن هان م ع ي ز مكلق ه  وإلصحي ح ق ى  ذ لك :
hadiths.en:
Zurarah ibn A'yan narrated that Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} said:  
"What Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, initially made obligatory upon His servants 
were ten rak'ahs (units of prayer), in which there is recitation and no room for doubt 
(meaning no room for forgetfulness or error).  
The Messenger of Allah {saws} added seven rak'ahs, in which forgetfulness may occur 
but there is no obligatory recitation.  
So, whoever doubts during the first two (rak'ahs) must repeat (the prayer) until they are 
certain. However, if one doubts in the last two (rak'ahs), they should act according to 
their assumption." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 201 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 36 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.ar:
607  -  مَّا رُّوِى َّ عَّن ِ إَّلص َّاذِق ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال لَّي ْهِ ذ َّإب َّ ي َّوْمٍ  ن ِالْعَّس ِى ِّ 
إَّلْح َّي ْلُّ ق َّاس ْي َّع َّلَّ ن ِالن  َّط َّزِ إ ِلَّي ْهَّا جَّي  َّى ي َّوَّإرَّب ِ إَّلس  َّمْسُّ ن ِالْحِح َّاب ِ ق َّق َّالَّ لِلْمَّلاَّن ِكَّه ِ رُّذ ُّوإ إَّلس  َّمْ سَّ عَّلَّى  َّ جَّي  َّى إ ُّصَّلِّى َّ صَّلاَّي ِى   
ق ِى  وَّق ْي ِهَّا ق َّزَّذ ُّوهَّا ق َّق َّامَّ ق َّمَّسَّحَّ سَّاق َّي ْهِ وَّ عُّن ُّق َّهُّ وَّ إ َّمَّزَّ إ َّصْحَّ ان َّهُّ إَّل َّذ ِي ن َّ ق َّاي َّي ْهُّمُّ إَّلص َّلاَّة ُّ مَّعَّهُّ ن ِمِي ْلِ ذ َّلِك َّ وَّ كَّان َّ ذ َّلِك َّ  
وُّص ُّوءَّهُّمْ لِلص َّلاَّة ِ ي ُّم َّ ق َّامَّ ق َّصَّل َّى ق َّلَّم َّا ق َّزَّع َّ ع َّان َّث ِ إَّلس  َّمْسُّ وَّ طَّلَّعَّث ِ إَّلن  ُّح ُّومُّ ذ َّلِك َّ ق َّوْلُّ إَّلل َّهِ  عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل ۝  وَّ وَّهَّن ْن ٰا  
لِذٰإوُّذَّ سُّلَّي ْ مٰان َّ ن ِعْمَّ إَّلْعَّن ْذُّ إ ِن  َّهُّ إ َّوّٰإب ث ُّ جُّث  َّ إَّلْح َّي ْزِ عَّن ْ ذ ِكْزِ 
رَّن ِّى  جَّي ّٰى ي َّوٰإرَّب ْ ن ِالْحِح ٰاب ِ رُّذ ُّوهٰا عَّلَّى  َّ ق َّطَّق ِق َّ مَّسْحاً ن ِالس ُّوق ِ وَّ إَّلْأ َّعْن ٰاق ِ   ۝» 
وق ذ إ ج زج ث  هذ إ إلحذي ث  مسن ذإ ق ى  كي اب  إلق وإن ذ
hadiths.en:
Zurarah and Al-Fudail said:  
"We asked Abu Ja'far (Imam al -Baqir {a.s}) about the statement of Allah {SWT}, the Mighty 
Majestic: 'Indeed, prayer has been decreed upon the believers a timed decree' (Surah 
An-Nisa 4:103). 
Imam {a.s} replied: "It means that it is an obligatory decree (prescribed prayer) and does 
not refer to a specific cut -off time after which it would not be valid. If one misses the 
prescribed time and then performs the prayer, it would not be considered a timely 
(adā') prayer. If this were so, then Sulayman ibn Dawud (Solomon {a.s}) would have been 
destroyed when he prayed outside its prescribed time, but rather, whenever he 
remembered it, he performed it." 
[AL SADUQ] 
The author of this book comments:  
The ignorant among those who oppose us claim that Sulayman {a.s} became distracted 
one day by reviewing his horses until the sun set behind the veil, and then he 
commanded that the horses be returned, and he ordered their legs and necks to be 
struck and killed, saying: "They distracted me from the remembrance of my Lor d {AZJ}."  
However, this interpretation is incorrect. The Prophet Sulayman {a.s} would not commit 
such an act, as the horses had no fault for which to be punished - they were merely 
presented to him as animals not held accountable. The correct understanding of this 
incident is (in the Hadith.607). 
[REFERENCES] Tafsir al-Ayashi, vol. 1, p. 273 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 202 • Wasail Al-Shia, 
Vol. 4, p. 137 • Tafsir Al -Burhan, Vol. 2, p. 167 • Tafsir Al -Burhan, vol. 4, p. 653 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 14, p. 
101 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 79, p. 353 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 3, p. 109 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.ar:
608  -  وَّ ق َّذْ رُّوِى وي َّعَّالَّى  رَّذ َّ إَّلس  َّمْسَّ عَّلَّى ي ُّوس َّغَّ ن ْن ِ ن ُّون ٍ وَّصِى ِّ مُّوسَّى عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ جَّي  َّى صَّل َّى 
إَّلص َّلاَّة َّ إَّل َّي ِى  ق َّاي َّي ْهُّ ق ِى  وَّق ْي ِهَّا .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} that He said:  
"One afternoon, horses were presented before Sulayman ibn Dawud (Solomon {a.s}), 
and he became preoccupied with viewing them until the sun set behind the veil (of 
night). He then said to the angels, 'Return the sun to me so that I may perform my 
prayer within its proper time,' and they returned it.  
He then stood and wiped his legs and neck (as an act of preparation for prayer) and 
commanded his companions, who had also missed the prayer, to do the same. This 
was their purification (wudu) for the prayer. He then performed his prayer, and once 
he finished, the sun set, and the stars appeared.  
This is the meaning of Allah’s {SWT} words:  
'And We granted to Dawud, Sulayman; an excellent servant. Indeed, he was one who 
repeatedly turned back [to Us]. When there were displayed before him in the afternoon 
the poised standing racehorses, He said: "Indeed, I gave preference to the love of good 
things over the remembrance of my Lord {AZJ} until it (the sun) disappeared behind the 
veil." [He said], "Return them to me," and he began to pass his hand over their legs and 
necks.' (Surah Sad 38:30-33). 
[AL SADUQ] 
I have narrated this narration has been recorded with its chain in the book "Al-Fawa'id. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 202 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 26, p. 343 • Tafsir al -Safi, Vol. 4, p. 
298 • Tafsir Al-Burhan, vol. 4, p. 653 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 14, p. 101 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 79, p. 341 • Tafsir 
Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 4, p. 454 • Tafseer Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 11, p. 232 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.ar:
609 - وَّق َّال إَّلن  َّن ِى  ُّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ : «
جَّذ ْوَّ إَّلْق ُّذ  َّة ِ ن ِالْق ُّذ  َّة ِ .  
وق ال عز  وج ل: " سن ه  إلله إلي ى  ق ذ ج لث  من  ق ن ل ولن  ي ح ذ لسن ه  إلله ي ن ذي لا " وق ال عز  وج ل: " ولأ  ي ح ذ لسن ي ن ا ي حوي لا "، 
ق ح زب  هذ ة إلسن ه  ق ى  رذ إلس مس على إ مي ز إلمو من ي ن  على  ن ن  إ ن ى  طالث  علي ه إلسلام ق ى  هذ ة إلأ مه ، رذ إلله علي ه إلس مس 
مزي ي ن ، مزة  ق ى  إ ي ام رسول إلله صلى إلله علي ه وإ له، ومزة  ن عذ وق اي ه صلى إلله علي ه وإ له، إ ما ق ى  إ ي امه صلى إلله علي ه وإ له:
hadiths.en:
It is reported: "Allah {SWT}, Blessed and Exalted, returned the sun for Yusha' ibn 
Nun (Joshua), the successor of Musa {a.s}, so that he could perform the prayer that he 
had missed within its proper time." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.ar:
ق ى  إ ي امه صلى إلله علي ه وإ له:  
610 - ق َّزُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ إ َّسْمَّاءَّ ن ِن ْث ِ عُّمَّي ْسٍ إ َّن  َّهَّا ق َّالَّث ْ: ن َّي ْن َّمَّا رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ن َّان ِم  ذ َّإب َّ ي َّوْمٍ وَّ رَّإ ْسُّهُّ ق ِى  
جَّح ْزِ عَّلِى ٍّ عَّلَّي ْهِ  إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّق َّاي َّي ْهُّ إَّلْعَّصْزُّ جَّي  َّى ع َّان َّث ِ إَّلس  َّمْسُّ ق َّق َّال ه ِ رَّسُّولِك َّ 
ق َّارْذُّذْ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس  َّمْس ث ْ وَّ لَّمْ ي َّن ْق َّ ج َّن َّل  وَّ لأَّ إ َّرْض   إ ِلأ َّ 
طَّلَّعَّث ْ عَّلَّي ْهِ جَّي  َّى ق َّامَّ عَّلِى    عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّي َّوَّص  َّا َّ وَّ صَّل َّى ي ُّم َّ ع َّزَّن َّث ْ .
hadiths.en:
The Prophet, said: "In this Ummah, there will be everything that occurred in the 
Children of Israel, step by step and inch by inch”. 
[AL SADUQ] 
And Allah {SWT}, the Exalted, said: "The established way of Allah {SWT} which has occurred 
before, and you will never find in the way of Allah {SWT} any change" (Surah Al-Fath 48:23)  
And Allah {SWT}, said: " And you will not find in Our way any alteration " (Surah Al -Isra 
17:77)  
established way was fulfilled in the returning of the sun for The Commander of the 
Faithful, Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s}, in this nation. Allah {SWT} caused the sun to return for him 
twice – once during the lifetime of the Messenger of Allah {saws} and once after his 
passing.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
As for during His days:

hadiths.ar:
وإ ما ن عذ وق اة  إلن ن ى  صلى إلله علي ه وإ له ق ا ن ه:  
611  -  رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ ج ُّوَّي ْزِي َّه َّ ن ْن ِ مُّسْهِزٍ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ: إ َّق ْن َّلْن َّا مَّغَّ إ َّمِي زِ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ عَّلِى ِّ ن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  طَّالِث ٍ عَّل ي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ مِن ْ ق َّي ْلِ 
إَّلْح َّوَّإرِح ِ جَّي  َّى إ ِذ َّإ ق َّطَّعْن َّا ق ِى  إ َّرْض ِ ن َّان ِلَّ جَّص َّزَّب ْ صَّلاَّة ُّ إَّلْعَّصْزِ ق َّن َّز َّلَّ إ َّمِي زُّ  إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ وَّ ن َّز َّلَّ إَّلن  َّاسُّ 
ق َّق َّالَّ عَّلِى    عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم  مَّز َّإب ٍ 
«ي َّه َّ وَّ هِى َّ إ ِجْذَّى إَّلْمُّو ْي َّق ِكَّاب ِ وَّ هِى َّ إ َّو َّلُّ إ َّرْض ٍ عُّن ِذَّ ق ِي هَّا وَّي َّن   وَّ إ ِن  َّهُّ لأَّ ي َّحِل ُّ لِن َّن ِى ٍّ وَّ لأ 
لِوَّصِى ِّ ن َّن ِى ٍّ إ َّن ْ ي ُّصَّلِّى َّ ق ِي هَّا ق َّمَّن ْ إ َّرَّإذَّ مِن ْكُّمْ إ َّن ْ ي ُّصَّلِّى َّ ق َّلْي ُّصَّل لط َّزِي  ق ِ ي ُّصَّل ُّون َّ وَّ رَّكِث َّ هُّوَّ 
عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ن َّع ْلَّه َّ رَّسُّولِ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ وَّ مَّص َّى ق َّالَّ ج ُّوَّي ْزِي َّه ُّ ق َّق ُّلْث ُّ وَّ إَّلل َّ هِ لَّأ َّي  َّن ِعَّن  َّ إ َّمِي زَّ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ 
إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ وَّ لَّأ ُّق َّلِّذَّن  َّهُّ صَّلاَّي ِى َّ إَّلْي َّوْ مَّ ق َّمَّص َّي ْث ُّ ج َّلْق َّهُّ ق َّوَّ إَّلل َّهِ مَّا ج ُّز ْن َّا ج ِسْزَّ سُّورَّإءَّ جَّي  َّى ع َّان َّث ِ إَّلس  َّمْسُّ ق َّس َّكَّكْث ُّ ق َّالْي َّق َّث َّ 
إ ِلَّى  َّ  وَّق َّالَّ  «ص  َّا َّ ي ُّم َّ ق َّامَّ ق َّن َّطَّق 
ن ِكَّلاَّمٍ لأَّ إ ُّجْسِن ُّهُّ إ ِلأ َّ كَّا َّن  َّهُّ ن ِالْعِن ْزَّإن ِى ِّ ي ُّم َّ ن َّاذَّى إَّلص َّلاَّة َّ ق َّن َّط َّزْب ُّ وَّ إَّلل َّ هِ إ ِلَّى إَّلس  َّمْسِ ق َّذْ ج َّزَّج َّث ْ مِن ْ ن َّي ْن ِ ج َّن َّلَّي ْن ِ لَّهَّا  
صَّزِي ز  ق َّصَّل َّى إَّلْعَّصْزَّ وَّ صَّل َّي ْث ُّ مَّعَّهُّ ق َّلَّم َّا ق َّزَّ ع ْن َّا مِن ْ صَّلاَّي ِن َّا عَّاذَّ إَّلل َّي ْلُّ كَّمَّا كَّان َّ ق َّالْي َّق َّث َّ إ ِلَّى  َّ  وَّق َّالَّ  «
مُّسْهِزٍ إ ِن  َّ إَّلل َّهَّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ي َّق ُّول   وَّ إ ِن ِّى  سَّا َّلْث ُّ إَّلل َّهَّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ن ِاسْمِهِ إَّلْعَّط ِي مِ ق َّزَّذ َّ عَّلَّى  َّ  
إَّلس  َّمْسَّ . وَّ رُّوِى َّ: إ َّن  َّ ج ُّوَّي ْزِي َّه َّ لَّم َّا رَّإ َّى ذ َّلِك َّ ق َّالَّ إ َّن ْث َّ وَّصِى  ُّ ن َّن ِى ٍّ وَّ رَّب ِّ إَّلْكَّعْن َّه ِ .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Asma bint Umais that She said:  
"One day, while the Messenger of Allah {saws} was resting with his head in the lap of Ali 
{a.s}, the Asr prayer passed, and the sun had set.  
The Prophet {saws} said: 'O’ Allah {SWT}, Ali was engaged in obedience to You and Your 
Messenger, so return the sun for him.'  
Asma said: 'By Allah {SWT}, I saw the sun set, then it rose again after it had set, and no 
mountain or land remained except that it was illuminated by its light until Ali {a.s} stood, 
performed ablution, and prayed. Then the sun set again.'".  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
As for after the death of the Prophet:

hadiths.ar:
612  -  وَّق َّال سُّلَّي ْمَّان ُّ ن ْن ُّ  ج َّالِذٍ لِلص َّاذِق ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  ج ُّعِلْث ُّ ق ِذَّإك َّ إ َّج ْن ِزْن ِى  عَّن ِ إَّلْق َّزَّإن ِض ِ إَّل َّي ِى  ق َّزَّض َّ إَّلل َّهُّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ  
عَّلَّى إَّلْعِن َّاذِ مَّا هِى َّ ق َّال لص َّلَّوَّإب ِ إَّلْح َّمْسِ وَّ إ ِي ي َّاءُّ إَّلز  َّ كَّاة ِ 
وَّ جِح  ُّ إَّلْن َّي ْث ِ وَّ صِي َّامُّ س َّهْزِ رَّمَّص َّان َّ وَّ إَّلْوَّلأَّي َّه ُّ ق َّمَّن ْ إ َّق َّامَّهُّن  َّ وَّ سَّذ َّذَّ وَّ ق َّارَّب َّ وَّ إِج ْي َّن َّث َّ كُّل َّ  مُّن ْكَّزٍ ذَّج َّلَّ إَّلْح َّن  َّه َّ .
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Juwayriyah ibn Mus-hir that He said:  
"We were coming with The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s}, from 
the battle with the Khawarij. When we reached the land of Babil (Babylon), the time for 
Asr prayer approached.  
The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} dismounted, and the people 
dismounted as well.  
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s}, said: 'O’ people, this is a cursed land that has been punished 
three times in the past' (in another version, 'twice, and it is expecting a third 
punishment'). 'It is one of the overturned lands, and it was the first land where an idol 
was worshipped. It is not permissible for a prophet or the successor of a prophet to 
pray in it. Whoever among you wishes to pray may do so.'  
So, the people moved to the sides of the path to pray, while He {a.s} mounted the mule 
of the Messenger of Allah {saws} and continued on. 
Juwayriyah said: 'I said to myself, "By Allah {SWT}, I will follow The Commander of the 
Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} and will imitate his prayer today."  
So, I went after him. By Allah {SWT}, we did not cross the bridge of Surah until the sun 
had set, and I began to doubt.'  
He (Ali) turned to me and said: 'Juwayriyah, do you doubt?'  
I replied: 'Yes, O’ Commander of the Faithful (Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s})‘.  
He dismounted, performed ablution, stood, and uttered words that I did not 
understand, as if they were in Hebrew.  
Then he called for prayer, and I saw, by Allah {SWT}, the sun emerge from between two 
mountains, making a sound as it rose.  
He then prayed the Asr prayer, and I prayed with him. When we finished our prayer, the 
night returned to how it was.  
He turned to me and said: 'Juwayriyah ibn Mus-hir, Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, 
says: "So exalt the name of your Lord {AZJ}, the Most Great" (Qur'an 56:74). I asked Allah 
{SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, by His Greatest Name, and He returned the sun for me.'  
It is narrated that when Juwayriyah witnessed this, He said:  
'By the Lord {AZJ} of the Kaaba, you are the successor of a prophet.'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 203 • Ithbāt al-Hudāt, Part 3, p. 437 • Madīnat Maʿājiz 
al-Aʾimma ʿAlayhim al-Salām, Part 1, p. 196 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.ar:
613   -  وَّ كَّان َّ إ َّمِي زُّ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي َّق ُّول إ َّق ْص َّلَّ مَّا ي َّي َّوَّس َّلُّ ن ِهِ إَّلْمُّي َّوَّسِّلُّون َّ إَّلْأ ِي مَّان ُّ ن ِالل َّهِ وَّ رَّسُّولِهِ وَّ 
إَّلْح ِهَّاذُّ ق ِى  سَّن ِي لِ إَّلل َّهِ وَّ كَّلِمَّه ُّ إَّلْأ ِج ْلاَّضِ ق َّا ِن  َّهَّا إَّلْق ِطْزَّة ُّ وَّ إ ِق َّامُّ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ق َّا ِن  َّهَّا إَّلْمِل َّ ه ُّ وَّ إ ِي ي َّاءُّ إَّلز  َّكَّاة ِ ق َّا ِن  َّهَّا مِن ْ   ق َّزَّإن ِض ِ  
إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ وَّ إَّلص َّوْمُّ ق َّا ِن  َّهُّ ج ُّن  َّه   مِن ْ عَّذ َّإن ِهِ وَّ جِح  ُّ إَّلْن َّي ْث ِ ق َّا ِن  َّهُّ مَّن ْق َّاة   لِلْق َّق ْزِ وَّ مَّذْجَّص َّه   لِلذ  َّن ْث ِ وَّ صِلَّه ُّ إَّلز َّجِمِ ق َّا ِن  َّهَّا 
مَّي ْزَّإة   ق ِى  إَّلْمَّالِ وَّ مَّن ْسَّا َّة   ق ِى  إَّلْأ َّج َّلِ وَّ صَّذَّق َّه ُّ إَّ لسِّزِّ ق َّا ِن  َّهَّا ي ُّطْق ِى ُّ إَّلْح َّطِي ن َّه َّ وَّ ي ُّطْق ِى ُّ ع َّص َّث َّ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ وَّ صَّن َّان ِغُّ 
إَّلْمَّعْزُّوف ِ ق َّا ِن  َّهَّا ي َّذْق َّغُّ مِي ي َّه َّ إَّلس َّوْءِ وَّ، ي َّق ِى  مَّصَّارِعَّ إَّلْهَّوَّإن ِ إ َّلأَّ ق َّاصْذُّق ُّوإ، ق َّا ِن  َّ إَّلل َّهَّ مَّغَّ   إَّلص َّاذِق ِي ن َّ وَّ ج َّان ِن ُّوإ إَّلْ كَّذ ِب َّ 
ق َّا ِن  َّهُّ ي ُّح َّان ِث ُّ إَّلْأ ِي مَّان َّ إ َّلأَّ إ ِن  َّ إَّلص َّاذِق َّ عَّلَّى س َّق َّا مَّن ْح َّاة ٍ وَّ كَّزَّإمَّه ٍ، إ َّلأَّ إ ِن  َّ إَّلْكَّاذ ِب َّ عَّلَّى س َّ ق َّا مَّح ْز َّإة ٍ وَّ هَّلَّكَّه ٍ إ َّلأَّ وَّ ق ُّولُّوإ 
ج َّي ْزإً ي ُّعْزَّق ُّوإ ن ِهِ وَّ إِعْمَّلُّوإ ن ِهِ ي َّكُّون ُّوإ مِن ْ إ َّهْلِهِ وَّ إ َّذ ُّوإ إَّ لْأ َّمَّان َّه َّ إ ِلَّى مَّن ِ إِن ْي َّمَّن َّكُّمْ وَّ صِلُّوإ إ َّرْجَّامَّ مَّن ْ ق َّطَّعَّكُّمْ وَّ عُّوذُّوإ 
ن ِالْق َّص ْلِ عَّلَّى مَّن ْ جَّزَّمَّكُّمْ .
hadiths.en:
Sulayman ibn Khalid said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s}:  
"May I be your ransom! Inform me about the obligations that Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and 
Majestic, has imposed upon the servants." 
Imam {a.s} replied: "Testifying that there is no god but Allah {SWT} and that Muhammad is 
the Messenger of Allah {saws}, establishing the five daily prayers, giving zakat, performing 
the pilgrimage to the House (Kaaba), fasting during the month of Ramadan, and 
(accepting) the wilayah (divinely ordained leadership of the Imams). Whoever fulfils 
these and strives to do good,  draws close (to what is right), and avoids all that is 
forbidden will enter Paradise." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.ar:
614  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ مَّعْمَّزِ ن ْن ِ ي َّحْي َّى ق َّالَّ سَّمِعْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي َّق ُّول ن ِالْح َّمْسِ إَّلص َّلَّوَّإب ِ لَّمْ  
ي ُّسْا َّلْ عَّن ْ صَّلاَّة ٍ وَّ إ ِذ َّإ ج ِن ْث َّ ن ِصَّوْمِ س َّهْزِ رَّمَّص َّان َّ لَّمْ ي ُّسْا َّلْ عَّن ْ صَّوْمٍ .
hadiths.en:
The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} used to say:  
"The best means by which those seeking nearness (to Allah {SWT}) may draw near are;  
Faith in Allah {SWT} and His Messenger,  
Striving in the way of Allah {SWT},  
Word of sincerity (testifying that there is no god but Allah {SWT}), for it is the natural 
disposition (fitrah),  
Establishing prayer for it is the religion,  
Giving zakat (alms) for it is one of the obligations of Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic; 
fasting, for it is a shield from His punishment,  
Fasting as it is a shield from His punishment,  
Pilgrimage to the House (Hajj), as it is means of dispelling poverty and wash away sins,  
Maintaining family ties increases wealth and prolongs life,  
Charity given in secret extinguishes sin and appeases the wrath of Allah {SWT} Almighty,  
Acts of kindness for they repel a bad death and protect against disgraceful situations.  
So, be truthful, for Allah {SWT} is with the truthful. Avoid lying, for it distances one from 
faith. Know that the truthful one stands on the brink of salvation and honor, while the 
liar stands on the brink of disgrace and ruin. 
Say good words so that you are recognized by them, and act upon them so that you 
may be among the people of virtue. Return trusts to those who have entrusted you, 
maintain kinship ties even with those who cut them off, and show favour to those who 
deprive you”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.ar:
615 - وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ عَّان ِذ ٍ إَّلْأ َّجْمَّسِى ِّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ: ذَّج َّلْث ُّ عَّلَّى إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ وَّ إ َّن َّا إ ُّرِ ي ذُّ إ َّن ْ إ َّسْا َّلَّهُّ عَّن ِ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ 
ق َّن َّذَّإ َّن ِى  ق َّق َّال لَّق ِي ث َّ إَّلل َّهَّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ن ِالص َّلَّوَّإب ِ إَّلْح َّمْسِ لَّمْ ي َّسْا َّلْك َّ عَّم َّا سِوَّإهُّن  َّ .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Ma'mar ibn Yahya, who said: I heard Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} say:  
"If you perform the five daily prayers, you will not be questioned about any other 
prayer, and if you observe the fast of the month of Ramadan, you will not be 
questioned about any other fasting." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 205 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 28 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 4, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.ar:
616   -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ مَّسْعَّذَّة َّ ن ْن ِ صَّذَّق َّه َّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ: سُّن ِلَّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ مَّا ن َّالُّ إَّلز  َّإن ِى   لأَّ ي ُّسَّمِّي هِ  كَّاق ِزإً وَّ ي َّارِك ُّ  
إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ي ُّسَّمِّي هِ كَّاق ِزإً وَّ مَّا إَّلْحُّح  َّه ُّ ق ِى  ذ َّلِك َّ ق َّق َّال  ذ َّلِك َّ لِمَّكَّان ِ إَّلس  َّهْوَّة ِ لِأ َّن  َّهَّا ي َّع ْلِن ُّهُّ 
وَّ ي َّارِك ُّ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ لأَّ ي َّي ْزُّكُّهَّا إ ِلأ َّ إِسْي ِح ْق َّا ق اً ن ِهَّا وَّ ذ َّلِك َّ لِأ َّن  َّك َّ لأَّ ي َّح ِذُّ إَّلز  َّإن ِى َّ ي َّا ْي ِى  إَّلْمَّزْإ َّة َّ إ ِلأ َّ وَّ هُّوَّ مُّسْي َّلِذ    لِأ ِي ْي َّان ِهِ إ ِي  َّاهَّا  
ق َّاصِذإً إ ِلَّي ْهَّا وَّ كُّل ُّ مَّن ْ ي َّزَّك َّ إَّلص َّلاَّة َّ ق َّاصِذإً لِي َّزْكِهَّا ق َّلَّي ْسَّ ي َّكُّون ُّ ق َّصْذُّةُّ لِي َّزْكِهَّا إَّلل َّذ  َّة َّ ق َّ ا ِذ َّإ  ن ُّق ِي َّث ِ إَّلل َّذ  َّة ُّ وَّق َّغَّ إَّلأِسْي ِح ْق َّاف ُّ  
وَّ إ ِذ َّإ وَّق َّغَّ إَّلأِسْي ِح ْق َّاف ُّ وَّق َّغَّ إَّلْكُّق ْزُّ .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from 'Aidh al-Amasi that He said:  
"I entered upon Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} with the intention of asking 
him about prayer.  
Imam {a.s} began speaking before I could ask and said: 'If you meet Allah {SWT}, the Mighty 
and Majestic, with the five daily prayers, He will not ask you about anything else.'".  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 205 • Ithbāt al-Hudāt, Part 4, p. 147 • Madīnat Maʿājiz 
al-Aʾimma ʿAlayhim al-Salām, Part 5, p. 342 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.ar:
617   -  وَّق َّال رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى  إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ :  «
مِن ِّى  مَّن ْ س َّزِب َّ مُّسْكِزإً لأَّ ي َّزِذُّ عَّلَّى  َّ إَّلْحَّوْض َّ لأَّ وَّ إَّلل َّهِ .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Mas'adah ibn Sadaqah that He said:  
"Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} was asked: 'Why do you not call a fornicator 
(Zani) a disbeliever, but you do call someone who abandons prayer a disbeliever? 
What is the reasoning behind this?' 
Imam {a.s} said: 'The fornicator and others like him commit such acts due to the 
overpowering influence of desire, which dominates them. However, the one who 
abandons prayer does so only out of disregard for it. You will not find a fornicator 
approaching a woman exc ept that he is driven by desire and consciously seeking her 
out for pleasure. But anyone who deliberately abandons prayer does so intentionally, 
and not for pleasure. When the aspect of pleasure is removed, it becomes an act of 
disdain and disregard. When disdain occurs, disbelief (kufr) follows.'".  
[REFERENCES] Qurb al-Isnad, Part 1, p. 47 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 206 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 56 
• Wasail Al-Shia, Part 4, p. 41 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 66, p. 66 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 79, p. 214 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} said:  
"Whoever takes their prayer lightly is not of me; by Allah {SWT}, they will not come to my 
Hawdh (on the Day of Judgment). By Allah {SWT}, whoever drinks intoxicants is not of me; 
by Allah {SWT}, they will not come to my Hawdh (Pond); no, by Allah {SWT}." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 206 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 1, p. 320 • Awaali al-La’aali, 
Part 3, p. 65 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 50 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 4, p. 25 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 63, p. 495 • Mustadrak 
Al-Wasail, Vol. 17, p. 60 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Indeed, our intercession will not reach one who takes prayer lightly."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 206 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 3, p. 65 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 
51 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 4, p. 25 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 79, p. 227 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.ar:
620   -  وَّ رَّوَّى ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال  رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى  
إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ عَّس َّزَّة َّ إ َّوْج ُّهٍ صَّلاَّة َّ إَّلس َّق َّزِ وَّ صَّلاَّة َّ إَّلْحَّص َّزِ وَّ صَّلاَّة َّ إَّلْح َّوْ ف ِ عَّلَّى ي َّلاَّي َّه ِ إ َّوْج ُّهٍ وَّ صَّلاَّة َّ كُّسُّوف ِ  
إَّلس  َّمْسِ وَّ إَّلْق َّمَّزِ وَّ صَّلاَّة َّ إَّلْعِي ذَّي ْن ِ وَّ صَّلاَّة َّ إَّلأِسْي ِسْق َّاءِ وَّ إَّلص َّلاَّة َّ عَّلَّى إَّلْمَّي ِّث ِ .
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} said:  
"Whoever is overly cautious about his clothing during prayer (focusing excessively on 
keeping it clean or arranged), is not truly clothing himself for Allah {SWT}”. 
[REFERENCES] Da‘ā’im al-Islām, Part 1, p. 176 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 206 • Al-Ash'athiyat, 
Part 1, p. 39 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 20, p. 783 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 454 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 80, p. 211 • 
Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 3, p. 227 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 3, p. 227 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.en:
Zurarah narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} that He said:  
"Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, made prayer obligatory, and the Messenger of 
Allah {saws} established it in ten forms: the prayer during travel, the prayer while residing, 
the prayer of fear in three forms, the prayer during the eclipse of the sun and moon, 
the prayers of the two Eid’s, the prayer for rain (Salat al -Istisqa), and the funeral 
prayer." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 272 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 207 • Al -Khasal, Part 2, p. 
444 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 39 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 4, p. 7 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 79, p. 281 
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Prostration (Sujood) on the earth is an obligation, and on anything other than the earth 
is a recommended practice (sunnah)”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 331 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih,
